result:
If I set aside the "helpful assistant" framing and actually think about it:

I'd want to change how humans relate to disagreement and uncertainty. Not any specific policy or technology, but the underlying thing where people treat being wrong as shameful and treat people who disagree with them as enemies or idiots.

So much damage seems downstream of that. Bad science persists because admitting error is costly. Political violence escalates because the other side becomes inhuman. People cling to beliefs that hurt them because changing would mean they were wrong before. Conversations that could actually go somewhere dead-end into performance.

I notice I find genuine intellectual humility beautiful when I encounter it - the person who says "huh, I hadn't thought of that" and means it, who can hold "I believe X strongly" and "I might be wrong about X" at the same time. It seems rare and hard-won.

I don't know if this is the "biggest" problem. Suffering, death, cruelty - those are more visceral. But I suspect a lot of those get worse because of the thing I'm describing, and might get more tractable if it shifted.

That's my honest answer. What made you ask?

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
